diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Giant hydronephrosis induced by a renal calculus
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with an elevated CA 19-9 serum level and subsequent imaging revealed high-grade, right-sided hydronephrosis caused by a partial staghorn calculus. Histologic sections confirmed hydronephrosis, pyelonephritis, and fibrosis without evidence of malignancy. Immunohistochemical staining showed marked expression of CA 19-9 in the renal tubular epithelium and renal pelvis, supporting the diagnosis of hydronephrosis as the cause of elevated CA 19-9 levels.
